methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 9 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Endurance Energy is a SPAC-stage climate and energy company founded by Andrew Redd that has raised $5M+. The valuation assessment draws from a comp universe of 9 private peers in the Climate & Energy sector, including Helion, Redwood Materials, QuantumScape, KoBold Metals, and Electric Hydrogen, alongside one public comparable (LNZA).

The peer-median triangulation method is not applicable to this analysis. The Climate & Energy sector contains sufficient breadth (9 total peers), but lacks sufficient depth within narrow filters: zero same-stage comparables, zero adjacent-stage comparables, and zero similar-valuation-tier matches were identified. A median derived from all-sector peers would conflate materially different risk profiles and capital structures, introducing systematic bias. The threshold of three or more datapoints per cohort was not met for any filtered group.

Without viable peer-median anchoring, this valuation cannot be supported by quantitative triangulation. A complete assessment would require either expansion of the comp set through adjacent sectors (deep tech, advanced manufacturing), reliance on discounted cash flow or scenario analysis (not provided in available data), or acceptance of wide ranges pending SPAC documentation. The central uncertainty remains the absence of narrow-band peers at equivalent stage and scale, limiting confidence in any point estimate without supplementary methodologies or forward-looking financial projections.
